title: Otimização de Rotas de Múltiplos Caminhos em Redes de Dados Nomeados Usando Vetor de Caminhos
author: SANTOS DOS SANTOS, FABIO
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Named data networks are an internet paradigm of the future characterized by their content-centricity, as opposed to the current architecture, TCP/IP, which is host-centric. Routing protocols in named data networks provide content reachability information, having an important application in this architecture, as it contributes with information that enables greater assertiveness in targeting the reach and return of data. In this context, the NDVR presents itself as a lightweight, safe tool with a simple and efficient synchronization process. NDVR provides support for multiple paths, but encounters difficulties in some topologies with route quality. This article proposes the use of path vectors in order to mitigate these problems. It shows positive results and presents in future work a scalable solution for the use of path vectors in large topologies.
